title: Kirtan Loor

{{Quote|Loor couldn't find his hands if you started him looking at his wrists.|Corran Horn|<ref name="Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: Family Ties">''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: Family Ties]]''</ref>}}
'''Kirtan Loor''' was an agent with [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] and former liaison officer to [[Corellian Security Force|CorSec]] who played a large role in the events surrounding the capture of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. In time, Loor would prove to be one of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron's]] most persistent enemies. Loor resembled a much taller and younger [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Tarkin]], and possessed an eidetic memory. These traits made Loor arrogant, making him feel superior to others.

==Biography==
===Early career===
In [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]]<ref name="KT">''[[X-Wing: The Krytos Trap]]''</ref> Kirtan Loor became an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial Liaison|liaison officer]] to [[Corellian Security Force|CorSec]]. He was assigned to the officers [[Gil Bastra]], [[Iella Wessiri Antilles|Iella Wessiri]], and [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]]. Loor was not interested in the internal affairs of CorSec, and even less in its officers. Loor also delighted in complicating simple cases, even to the point of letting [[Bossk/Legends|the killer]] of [[Hal Horn|Horn's father]] go free.

After enduring years of abuse under Loor, Bastra, Wessiri, and Horn hatched a plot to escape Loor. After they escaped right under his nose, Loor left his post as liaison officer, vowing to track down the three former CorSec officers and have his revenge.

He almost caught up with Corran Horn on the planet [[Garqi/Legends|Garqi]]—Corran Horn was posing as Eamon Yzalli, aide to Prefect [[Mosh Barris/Legends|Mosh Barris]] as one of the aliases created by Gil Bastra. Horn was involved in a [[Great Evacuation|plot]] to free the [[Duro/Legends|Duros]] prisoner [[Lai Nootka]], who had some valuable parts for Horn's [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]]. Horn framed Mosh Barris for his "murder," and Loor was sent to investigate. Horn and Loor missed each other by a matter of hours.

Three months after the events on Garqi, Loor tracked down and captured Gil Bastra, though Bastra had insisted that he had let Loor catch him on purpose, to keep pursuit off his friends. Loor attempted to interrogate Bastra, but when he put Bastra in a [[Bacta tank/Legends|bacta tank]] to cure him of a fatal disease, he failed to realize that Bastra was [[Allergy/Legends|allergic]] to [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]]. Bastra died in the interrogation attempt, leaving Loor back where he started. At that time, [[Ysanne Isard]] recalled him to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].

===Employment under Isard===
[[File:Kirtanloor-jpn.jpg|thumb|left|120px|Loor in 6.5 ABY]]

After his recall to Coruscant, Loor met Ysanne Isard, [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]]. Isard recruited Loor and ordered him to track down and destroy [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]. Why Loor was chosen was unknown, though it was suspected that Isard knew of his vendetta against Corran Horn, who at the time was a member of Rogue Squadron.

Working with Admiral [[Devlia]], Loor tracked down Rogue Squadron to their base on [[Talasea]]. Fortunately for Rogue Squadron, Devlia was skeptical and mistrusted Loor's abilities. He sent only a single squad of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], who were easily dispatched by Rogue Squadron, but one of the squadron's pilots, [[Lujayne Forge]], was killed in the attack. When the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] retaliated and attacked Devlia's headquarters on [[Vladet]], Loor made his escape. He was then ordered to go to [[Borleias/Legends|Borleias]], which was a vital stepping stone for the New Republic to reach Coruscant.

After his arrival, Loor met with General [[Evir Derricote]]. Loor warned Derricote about the inevitable New Republic attack, and also spied on Derricote. After Derricote repelled the first New Republic assault, Isard ordered Loor to bring Derricote back to Coruscant with him.

===Events on Coruscant===
After the New Republic captured Borleias and inserted Rogue Squadron into Coruscant, Loor was again tasked with fighting off the squadron, though this time, he was only supposed to delay them until Isard could unleash her lethal [[Krytos virus]] on the planet's [[Alien/Legends|nonhuman]] population and put pressure on the New Republic. To this end, Loor enlisted the help of [[Zekka Thyne]] and [[Erisi Dlarit]] to spy on Rogue Squadron. Unfortunately for him, tough anti-espionage tactics employed by [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] prevented him from stopping the New Republic's premature capture of Coruscant.

[[File:Loor_isard.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Loor and [[Ysanne Isard]]]]

As the New Republic closed in, Isard informed Loor that he was to lead the [[Palpatine Counter-Insurgency Front]] and perform guerrilla and terrorist attacks on the new government. In this role, Loor found himself working for [[Fliry Vorru]], a former [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]]. Vorru had Loor attack multiple targets, including a bacta storage facility and a school.

Loor was not happy with any of this, and secretly plotted to overthrow Vorru and Isard. When he learned of a convoy that was going to deliver bacta to the New Republic, Loor took the chance. Using twelve [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] he managed to obtain, Loor intended to steal the bacta for himself and blame the theft on Rogue Squadron. However, Isard also knew of the convoy and tipped off [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]] about it. Zsinj destroyed the entire convoy, including Loor's fake Rogue Squadron.

With his plan in shambles, Loor was sure that Isard would discover his plans for betrayal. He sought sanctuary with the New Republic by offering to testify in [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu's]] trial and divulging information about all Imperial activities on Coruscant. During his defection, he was codenamed "Behemoth." Loor never got the chance to reap the rewards of his defection, however. Isard received word of a secret witness attending Celchu's trial. Thinking it was General Derricote, Isard sent [[Diric Wessiri]], a sleeper agent and Iella Wessiri's husband, to prevent him from testifying. As Loor was on his way to the courthouse, he was shot by Diric and died at the scene.

===Loor's legacy===
Although Isard kept him quiet, Loor got the last laugh. Before he died, Loor kept an encoded [[Datacard/Legends|datacard]] with him as a fail-safe to prevent the New Republic from just killing him. When Horn discovered the card, he decoded the data (encrypted using the [[Xucphra Corporation]]'s stock listing on the [[Imperial Stock Exchange]]<ref name="KT" />), which revealed every Imperial operation, terrorist cell, and safe house on Coruscant. With Loor's help, the New Republic went a long way toward making Coruscant more secure for the future.
